description: Lead's optional additions on top of the post-verdict state. Empty arrays are the common case — silence is fine.

corrections.description: Your own additions to the writer's punch list — concerns every reviewer missed.

verdict.description: fix = writer wrote something wrong; missing = writer left a needed action out; remove = writer included something out of scope.

action_id.description: Required for fix and remove — the action id being fixed or removed, e.g. v84-1.3.frontend.1.

task_id.description: Required for missing — the task id an action is missing under, e.g. v84-1.3.

correction.description: Concise prose (1–3 sentences) stating the break and the change to make.

rules.description: Rules you'd enact for this role. Lead is the role's authority — these settle as accepted on the spot.

alternatives.description: 1–3 other viable forms of the same rule.

examples.title: One or more concerns to raise

corrections.correction: Add a prefers-reduced-motion media query that disables every keyframe animation.

rules.proposal: Every CSS animation block is wrapped in @media (prefers-reduced-motion: no-preference).
alternatives: All animations gated by a single :root custom property; toggle from one place.
Animations always on; provide a small UI control to pause.
